titles.title: Pyphot: A tool for computing photometry from spectra

institution.institution_name: Max Planck Institute for Astronomy, Königstuhl 17, 69117 Heidelberg, Germany
Université de Strasbourg, CNRS, Observatoire astronomique de Strasbourg, UMR 7550, F-67000 Strasbourg, France

citation.author: Rodrigo
citation.journal_title: XIV.0 scientific meeting (virtual) of the spanish astronomical society

citation.article_title: Astropy: A community Python package for astronomy
citation.author: Astropy Collaboration
citation.unstructured_citation: Astropy Collaboration, Robitaille, T. P., Tollerud, E. J., Greenfield, P., Droettboom, M., Bray, E., Aldcroft, T., Davis, M., Ginsburg, A., Price-Whelan, A. M., Kerzendorf, W. E., Conley, A., Crighton, N., Barbary, K., Muna, D., Ferguson, H., Grollier, F., Parikh, M. M., Nair, P. H., … Streicher, O. (2013). Astropy: A community Python package for astronomy. Astronomy & Astrophysics, 558, A33. https://doi.org/10.1051/0004-6361/201322068

citation.article_title: Old stellar populations. V. Absorption feature indices for the complete Lick/IDS sample of stars
citation.author: Worthey
citation.unstructured_citation: Worthey, G., Faber, S. M., Gonzalez, J. J., & Burstein, D. (1994). Old stellar populations. V. Absorption feature indices for the complete Lick/IDS sample of stars. The Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series, 94, 687. https://doi.org/10.1086/192087

citation.article_title: Evolutionary stellar population synthesis with MILES - I. The base models and a new line index system
citation.author: Vazdekis
citation.journal_title: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society
citation.unstructured_citation: Vazdekis, A., Sánchez-Blázquez, P., Falcón-Barroso, J., Cenarro, A. J., Beasley, M. A., Cardiel, N., Gorgas, J., & Peletier, R. F. (2010). Evolutionary stellar population synthesis with MILES - I. The base models and a new line index system. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 404(4), 1639–1671. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2966.2010.16407.x

citation.article_title: An optimized H\beta index for disentangling stellar population ages
citation.author: Cervantes
citation.journal_title: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society
citation.unstructured_citation: Cervantes, J. L., & Vazdekis, A. (2009). An optimized H\beta index for disentangling stellar population ages. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 392(2), 691–704. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2966.2008.14079.x

citation.article_title: Constraints on stellar populations in elliptical galaxies.
citation.author: Rose
citation.unstructured_citation: Rose, J. A. (1985). Constraints on stellar populations in elliptical galaxies. The Astronomical Journal, 90, 1927–1956. https://doi.org/10.1086/113898

citation.article_title: The sensitivity of Lick indices to abundance variations
citation.author: Korn
citation.unstructured_citation: Korn, A. J., Maraston, C., & Thomas, D. (2005). The sensitivity of Lick indices to abundance variations. Astronomy & Astrophysics, 438(2), 685–704. https://doi.org/10.1051/0004-6361:20042126

citation.article_title: H\gamma and H\delta absorption features in stars and stellar populations
citation.author: Worthey
citation.unstructured_citation: Worthey, G., & Ottaviani, D. L. (1997). H\gamma and H\delta absorption features in stars and stellar populations. The Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series, 111(2), 377–386. https://doi.org/10.1086/313021

citation.article_title: Flux-calibrated stellar population models of Lick absorption-line indices with variable element abundance ratios
citation.author: Thomas
citation.journal_title: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society
citation.unstructured_citation: Thomas, D., Maraston, C., & Johansson, J. (2011). Flux-calibrated stellar population models of Lick absorption-line indices with variable element abundance ratios. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 412(4), 2183–2198. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-2966.2010.18049.x

citation.article_title: The stellar IMF in early-type galaxies from a non-degenerate set of optical line indices
citation.author: Spiniello
citation.journal_title: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society
citation.unstructured_citation: Spiniello, C., Trager, S., Koopmans, L. V. E., & Conroy, C. (2014). The stellar IMF in early-type galaxies from a non-degenerate set of optical line indices. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 438(2), 1483–1499. https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/stt2282
